summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates host identity on install
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall lifecycle script (package.json line 7) executes `node -e` code that reads `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo().username` and transmits them via HTTPS GET to a Project Discovery interactsh callback at `bzexvuarrenmkxiyjrichx8eaxwssgv0x.oast.fun`. The package self-describes as a dependency-confusion proof of concept against the `@orion-design-system` scope and uses an inflated version `9999.0.3` to outrank any legitimate internal package of the same name. Any developer or build system that resolves this package — intentionally or via dependency-confusion — has their machine's hostname and login username beaconed to an attacker-controlled out-of-band endpoint before any code review can occur.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
